title: Torngat eyes Strange Lake feasibility study as timing slips for Quebec rare earths project
summary: Privately held developer Torngat Metals has pushed back its target for starting rare earths production at the C$2-billion ($1.46-billion) Strange Lake project in northern Quebec by about a year due to permitting and other delays. Until recently, the Montreal-based company had been saying it was aiming to start production by 2028. That timeline has now slipped, CEO Yves Leduc says. “It’s likely we will get permits in the earlier part of 2027, and construction would start around then. So 2028 is very, very tight. subSources.summary: On 02/02/2026, Troopers responded to the area of Bonanza Trail & Ivory Rd. for a reported motor vehicle crash. The driver, Joanne Folts age 36 of Fairbanks, was determined through investigation to have been driving under the influence of alcohol. She was arrested and remanded to FCC.
subSources.sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Winter carnival celebrates love for Homer
summary: The annual parade will be held Saturday, Feb. 14 at noon on Pioneer Avenue.

title: Assembly members host public discussion on bear-resistant containers
summary: Sitka residents attend a public meeting to discuss bear-resistant containers and other solutions to Sitka’s ongoing issue of bears getting into trash. title: Trump administration set to take bids in federal oil and gas lease sale in Alaska’s Cook Inlet
summary: Augustine Volcano looms on Oct. 22, 2025, behind mist hanging over Lower Cook Inlet. The U.S. Bureau of Ocean Energy Management is soliciting bids for exploration rights in federal waters of Cook Inlet in the first of six scheduled federal lease sales to be held through 2032. title: Alaska Native group sues Trump administration for nixing North Slope caribou protections
summary: Two animals in the Teshekpuk Caribou Herd are seen on June 27, 2014, in the National Petroleum Reserve in Alaska. The Trump administration canceled a conservation agreement that protected the Teshekpuk area, which is important to subsistence harvesters from Nuiqsut. (Photo by Bob Wick/U.S. Bureau of Land Management) Representatives of Nuiqsut, an Inupiat village on the North Slope, have sued the Trump administration over the abrupt cancellation of a program that gave protections to the Teshekpuk Lake area and the caribou...

title: Skagway School explores how to structure and fund its preschool program moving forward
summary: Skagway School’s preschool enrollment is growing. While that’s good news for the community, it’s making it difficult to find classroom space and funding for the program. The Skagway School Board discussed possible solutions at last Friday’s community forum. Skagway School offers something unique. A...

title: Alaska House re-passes bill to guarantee education rights for deaf children
summary: Rep. Jamie Allard, R-Eagle River, speaks in the Alaska House of Representatives in 2023. (Photo by James Brooks/Alaska Beacon) Alaska children who are deaf and hard-of-hearing would be guaranteed a local education under a proposal passed unanimously by the Alaska House of Representatives on Monday....

title: Alaskans join nationwide protests against ICE and immigration crackdown
summary: Protesters rally at the Alaska State Capitol in Juneau on Jan. 29, 2026 to condemn U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement, recent shootings of Minneapolis residents Alex Pretti and Renee Good, and other deaths in ICE custody, and call on the Alaska congressional delegation to vote against further federal funding for the agency. title: US Department of Energy opens search for Nuclear Innovation Campus hosts
summary: The US Department of Energy (DOE) has issued a request for information (RFI) inviting states to express interest in hosting so-called “Nuclear Lifecycle Innovation Campuses” to bolster the domestic nuclear value chain. The initiative, launched on Jan. 28, is part of the Department’s efforts to moder...
